body.title: CLOSED: Librarian memory curation — standalone reasoner in ADC librarian device
body.result: curation_tools.py created with duplicate/stale/code_ref detection; writes archive_action proposals; 9 tests green
body.description:
Add memory curation capability to the existing Librarian device (`agent_datacenter/devices/librarian/`). New module `tools/curation_tools.py`. Reads clan.memories via direct psycopg2, reasons about what's redundant/stale/underused, writes proposals to instance.proposals (T-igor-proposals-queue pattern). The Librarian is the correct host: standalone reasoner, independently debuggable, no Igor brain overhead. Design principle: Librarian PROPOSES, Igor DECIDES.

Curation logic (first pass): (1) near-duplicate FACTUAL/PROCEDURAL entries (same code_ref or narrative md5 fingerprint); (2) SEMANTIC memories with no access in 30 days; (3) PROCEDURAL habits sharing code_ref with different conditions. Each finding becomes one proposals row (kind=archive_action, content=JSON with reason+target_ids+proposed_action). Log all analysis to `datacenter_logs/librarian/curation.jsonl`.

Replaces T-igor-memory-librarian (incorrectly targeted at Igor's cognition stack — that ticket is closed/redirected here).

**Affected files:** `agent_datacenter/devices/librarian/tools/curation_tools.py` (new), `agent_datacenter/devices/librarian/librarian.py` (wire curation trigger), `tests/test_librarian_curation.py` (new)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; direct psycopg2; never hard-delete; all curation proposals via instance.proposals; curation log append-only JSONL; no cortex.py touch; Librarian is outside Igor entirely
**Scope boundary:** IN — curation analysis, proposals generation, log. OUT — no auto-commit, no cross-instance curation, no Igor internals.
**Test plan:** curation_tools finds seeded duplicate pair → archive_action proposal; stale memory (>30d no access) → prune proposal; recently-accessed memory NOT flagged; all proposals have source_module='librarian_curation'
